info.name: Snowflake Event Table API
info.description:
The Snowflake Event Table API is a REST API that you can use to access, update, and perform certain actions on Event Table resource in a Snowflake database.

Contact Support:
 Name: Snowflake, Inc.
 Email: support@snowflake.com

variable.description: Identifier (i.e. name) for the database to which the resource belongs. You can use the `/api/v2/databases` GET request to get a list of available databases.
Identifier (i.e. name) for the schema to which the resource belongs. You can use the `/api/v2/databases/{database}/schemas` GET request to get a list of available schemas for the specified database.
Identifier (i.e. name) for the resource.

response.name: successful

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

variable.description: Identifier (i.e. name) for the database to which the resource belongs. You can use the `/api/v2/databases` GET request to get a list of available databases.

response.body:
{
  "name": "string",
  "cluster_by": [
    "string",
    "string"
  ],
  "data_retention_time_in_days": 2957,
  "max_data_extension_time_in_days": 3083,
  "change_tracking": true,
  "default_ddl_collation": "string",
  "comment": "string",
  "created_on": "1970-06-03T05:58:33.241Z",
  "database_name": "string",
  "schema_name": "string",
  "owner": "string",
  "owner_role_type": "string",
  "rows": 2100,
  "bytes": 3314,
  "automatic_clustering": false,
  "search_optimization": true,
  "search_optimization_progress": 8438,
  "search_optimization_bytes": 8098,
  "columns": [
    {
      "name": "string",
      "datatype": "string",
      "nullable": false,
      "default": "string",
      "primary_key": false,
      "unique_key": true,
      "check": "string",
      "expression": "string",
      "comment": "string"
    },
    {
      "name": "string",
      "datatype": "string",
      "nullable": false,
      "default": "string",
      "primary_key": true,
      "unique_key": false,
      "check": "string",
      "expression": "string",
      "comment": "string"
    }
  ]
}

Parameter that specifies how to handle the request for a resource that does not exist:
- `true`: The endpoint does not throw an error if the resource does not exist. It returns a 200 success response, but does not take any action on the resource.
- `false`: The endpoint throws an error if the resource doesn't exist.

query.description:
Parameter allowing support for different modes of resource creation. Possible values include:
- `errorIfExists`: Throws an error if you try to create a resource that already exists.
- `orReplace`: Automatically replaces the existing resource with the current one.
- `ifNotExists`: Creates a new resource when an alter is requested for a non-existent resource.
Parameter to enable copy grants when creating the object.

response.name: Forbidden. The request is forbidden. This can also happen if the request is made even if the API is not enabled.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

query.description:
Parameter allowing support for different modes of resource creation. Possible values include:
- `errorIfExists`: Throws an error if you try to create a resource that already exists.
- `orReplace`: Automatically replaces the existing resource with the current one.
- `ifNotExists`: Creates a new resource when an alter is requested for a non-existent resource.
Parameter to enable copy grants when creating the object.
